related-application.description: Refurbishment and replacement of existing windows with timber slimline double-glazed units.

person.address-text: Flat 6 Cityscape Apartment, 43 Heneage St, London

nm-amendment-details.description: To amend the description of development to capture the heat pump as previously approved.
nm-amendment-details.reason:
The approved application included provision for an air source heat pump shown on the submitted drawings; however, it was not explicitly referenced within the description of development.

Following discussions with the case officer, it is understood that an amendment to the approved description is required to enable the proposed relocation of the heat pump to be considered through a separate Section 73 (variation of condition) application.

This Non-Material Amendment is therefore sought solely to update the description of development to accurately reflect the approved drawings and facilitate the validation and determination of the subsequent application.

pre-app-advice.advice-summary:
Regarding the current VOC application at 77 Stoke Newington Church Street, having reviewed the submission alongside the approved planning permission and listed building consent, I have identified a procedural requirement. Whilst the physical relocation of the heat pump cannot be treated as a Non-Material Amendment (NMA), we do require an NMA application to be submitted to amend the approved description of development.

Essentially, the current description does not explicitly reference the heat pump, despite it appearing on the approved drawings. As a Variation of Condition (VOC) application cannot be used to alter a description of development, an NMA must be submitted to rectify this before the VOC can be formally determined.
